data.title: Opera says co-CEO Lin Song to become sole CEO
data.description:
• Opera (NASDAQ:OPRA) said on Friday that co-CEO Lin Song will become the browser and artificial intelligence company's sole CEO, with co-CEO James Yahui Zhou stepping down as previously announced.
  • Zhou, who left the CEO role, will continue as Opera's Executive Chairman, the company said.
  • Opera shares fell 0.8% in premarket trading and are down 11.8% year-to-date.

data.title: Serve Robotics shares fall on $100M direct offering of common stock
data.description:
• Serve Robotics (NASDAQ:SERV) said on Friday that it has entered into securities purchase agreements with certain institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 6.25M shares of common stock, pursuant to a registered direct offering, expected to result in gross proceeds of approximately $100M.  
  • The company intends to use net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including working capital. 
  • Serve Robotics (NASDAQ:SERV) shares were down more than 7% in premarket trading.

data.title: Why sourcing rare earth minerals is key for electric vehicle makers
data.description:
China tightened its rare earth export rules on Thursday, the elements that are crucial to the manufacture of many high-tech products. The announcement from the Ministry of Commerce clarifies and expands sweeping controls announced in April, which had caused massive shortages around the world before a series of deals with Europe and the U.S. resumed shipments.

Rare earth minerals are crucial for electric vehicles because they are essential components in the powerful and efficient magnets used within EV propulsion motors. The magnets, typically composed of neodymium-iron-boron (NdFeB) or samarium-cobalt (SmCo), are the key to maximizing motor performance because they provide high-energy efficiency, compact size, and strong torque, which are all attributes vital for modern EV drivetrains. 

To simplify the importance, rare earth magnets allow EVs to be smaller, lighter, and more energy-efficient compared to motors that use less powerful magnets.

Major automakers source most of their rare earth minerals from China, which overwhelmingly dominates global production and processing of these materials. Notably, China supplies over 60% of the world’s mined rare earths and controls around 90% of the processing capacity. General Motors (GM), Ford Motor (F), and Rivian Automotive (RIVN) usually obtain rare earth minerals through suppliers who hold Chinese export licenses, although recent export restrictions or licensing changes could trigger supply disruptions. Meanwhile, Lucid Motors (LCID) is trying to be proactive by actively building domestic supply chains for rare earth minerals through a partnership with U.S.-based mineral producers, including Alaska Energy Metals, Graphite One, and Electric Metals, as part of the Minerals for National Automotive Competitiveness Collaboration.

As for Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives thinks the electric vehicle giant's massive presence in China gives it a clear advantage in sourcing rare earth minerals and magnets. "While Tesla could still get dented by this raw material tightening in China, the company is best positioned among US automakers to navigate any speed bumps on this front," he wrote.

Related ETFs include the VanEck Rare Earth and Strategic Metals ETF (NYSEARCA:REMX) and the Global X Lithium & Battery Tech ETF (NYSEARCA:LIT).

data.title: SEALSQ set for 3rd day of gains after 41% revenue growth, new U.S. defense partnership
data.description:
SEALSQ Corp. (NASDAQ:LAES) jumped over 11% in premarket trading on Friday, on track for a third straight day of gains.

Seal’s (NASDAQ:LAES) preliminary financials for 9M 2025 revealed revenue of $9.9 million (41% increase in year-on-year revenue growth), with $5.1 million recorded in the third quarter of this year, as per a filing. 

This 41% growth is mostly attributable to the expected return to growth in the demand for the company's traditional products and two months of consolidated revenue from IC’ALPS since the completion of the acquisition on August 4, 2025.

However, revenue is still negatively impacted by the ongoing transition from legacy products to next-generation post-quantum semiconductors and software, SEALSQ (NASDAQ:LAES) said in a statement.

Looking at the full-year revenue, it is expected to be between $17.5 million and $20.0 million, marking 59%–82% growth over 2024.

Separately, the company, on October 9, announced a strategic partnership to co-develop “Made in US” Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC)-enabled secure semiconductor solutions, reaching the highest levels of hardware certification tailored for US defense and government agencies.

data.title: Bristol Myers Squibb to buy Orbital Therapeutics for $1.5B in cash
data.description:
Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) has agreed to acquire privately held biotechnology company Orbital Therapeutics for $1.5 billion in cash.

Orbital Therapeutics is advancing a new generation of RNA medicines designed to reprogram cells in vivo, treating diseases at their source. Its lead RNA immunotherapy preclinical candidate, OTX-201, targets autoimmune disease through B cell depletion to reset the immune system.

In addition to OTX-201, the deal covers Orbital’s proprietary RNA platform, which integrates circular and linear RNA engineering, advanced lipid nanoparticles delivery, and AI-driven design to enable durable, programmable RNA therapies tailored to the distinct biology of a broad spectrum of diseases.

The deal strengthens Bristol Myers Squibb’s cell therapy portfolio. “With the acquisition of Orbital Therapeutics and its next-generation RNA platform, we have an incredible opportunity to make CAR T-cell therapy more efficient and accessible to more patients,” said Lynelle B. Hoch, president, Cell Therapy Organization, Bristol Myers Squibb.

data.title: Wallbox N.V. strikes a standstill agreement with key lenders
data.description:
Wallbox N.V. (NYSE:WBX) disclosed in an SEC filing on Friday that the EV charging company reached a standstill agreement with major lenders, including Banco Santander, BBVA, and CaixaBank, to pause principal and interest payments until December 9 or until a new long-term capital structure is finalized. 

The standstill agreement formalizes previous waivers and allows for payment defaults during discussions. Other lenders, such as Instituto de Crédito Oficial and COFIDES, are expected to join the agreement within one month, with negotiations already underway. If some minority lenders do not join by the deadline, the major lenders may end the agreement. 

The standstill agreement was struck to give Wallbox (NYSE:WBX) more time and flexibility to work out its financial structure with its creditors.

Shares of Wallbox (WBX) have perked up this week with a 27.4% rally, although the stock is still down more than 33% on a year-to-date basis and trades more than 70% below its 52-week high.

data.title: Tesla's China shipments rise in September
data.description:
Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) Shanghai factory posted higher shipments in September as China’s auto market entered its peak sales season and carmakers accelerated efforts to meet year-end goals.

The electric vehicle maker delivered 90,812 vehicles from its Shanghai plant last month, up 2.8% from a year earlier, according to preliminary data from the China Passenger Car Association. Although the data doesn’t separate exports from domestic sales, most vehicles are expected to have gone to Chinese buyers. The gain marks a break from a sluggish year — Tesla’s wholesale output from Shanghai has fallen in seven of the past nine months.

The strong month in China helped power a record third quarter for Tesla, which recently reported record global deliveries as U.S. consumers rushed to take advantage of expiring EV tax credits before Sept. 30.

While it remains uncertain if Tesla can sustain its momentum, the robust September showing in China points to positive demand heading into the fourth quarter. The Golden Week holiday at the start of October will serve as a key demand indicator for the rest of the year. Tesla has also begun delivering a six-seat version of its Model Y SUV to appeal to family buyers.

Meanwhile, BYD Co. (OTCPK:BYDDF) (OTCPK:BYDDY), China’s top-selling car brand and Tesla’s biggest rival, saw a year-on-year decline in September sales - the first in more than 18 months. BYD’s third-quarter deliveries slipped 1.3%, marking its first quarterly drop since 2020 — though the Shenzhen-based automaker has still outsold Tesla in pure EVs for the past four quarters.

data.title: BIT Mining rebrands to SOLAI Limited, shifts focus to AI and blockchain synergy
data.description:
• BIT Mining Limited (NYSE:BTCM) has officially changed its name to SOLAI Limited, following shareholder approval at its October 9, 2025.
  • The company will also change its ticker symbol to "SLAI", effective Monday, October 20, 2025.
  • The rebranding reflects a strategic pivot from cryptocurrency mining to AI-blockchain technology infrastructure.
  • BTCM shares up 4.2% premarket.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Gunnison Copper launches C$15M private placement
data.description:
• Gunnison Copper (TSX:GCU:CA) (OTCQB:GCUMF) has announced a non-brokered private placement to raise up to C$15M.
  • The offering includes up to 33.33M units priced at C$0.45 per unit.
  • Each unit consists of one common share and half of a warrant; each full warrant is exercisable at C$0.65 for 36 months.
  • Proceeds will fund drilling, metallurgical testing, permitting, and support a pre-feasibility study for the Gunnison Copper Project.
  • Additional funds will be used for U.S. head office expenses, partial debt repayment to Nebari, and general working capital.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Tom Lee’s Bitmine Immersion adds $103M more in ethereum
data.description:
• Tom Lee-backed Bitmine Immersion (NYSE:BMNR) purchased over $103 million in ETH, expanding its Ethereum (ETH-USD) treasury holdings to $12.6 billion.
  • The company scooped up another 23,823 ETH (worth about $103.68 million) from BitGo, according to Arkham data shared by Lookonchain on October 10.
  • The latest purchase expanded Bitmine’s total Ethereum holdings to 2.87 million ETH worth $12.6 billion at the current market price.
